content.text: Nouns are names of things or ideas.  For example, the noun 'cars' refers to things and the word 'love' refers to an idea or concept.

Number means whether a noun is singular, referring to one thing, or plural, referring to more than one thing. Unlike some other languages, there is no special form for a pair of things. For this reason, in both Greek and English, the noun itself is always either singular or plural.

English nouns are formed regularly with an s ending, for example: one car and two cars.  We also have irregular nouns, for example: One person and two people.  The Greek nouns we will learn first are entirely regular.  They add specific endings that show whether the noun is singular or plural, similar to the English 's' marker for plural nouns.  We will study the irregular nouns later on.
